name: Thermal WIMP freeze-out
summary: A weakly interacting massive particle in thermal equilibrium with the early universe annihilates via electroweak-strength couplings; equilibrium freezes out when annihilation rate falls below Hubble rate. For sigma_v ~ 3e-26 cm^3/s and m_X ~ 100 GeV - few TeV, the relic abundance lands on the observed Omega_DM h^2 ~ 0.12 (the 'WIMP miracle').

addresses_puzzles.note: The WIMP miracle is a numerical coincidence; many BSM frameworks (MSSM, composite Higgs, IDM) produce viable WIMP DM candidates. Direct-detection nulls (LZ, XENONnT) have squeezed canonical thermal WIMP parameter space, especially for spin-independent cross-sections.

touches_benchmarks.note: LZ 2024 spin-independent cross-section bounded < 9.2e-48 cm^2 at m_chi ~ 36 GeV (PRL); vanilla thermal Higgs-portal WIMPs largely excluded above 9 GeV/c^2.
Fermi dwarf-galaxy bounds rule out thermal WIMPs below ~100 GeV in s-wave annihilation channels.

new_fields: WIMP X (specific identity model-dependent: neutralino, scalar singlet, vector-like fermion)
new_symmetries: stabilising Z_2 (R-parity, dark parity) or accidental
introduces.note: Mass, coupling, and stabilisation; specific UV completions add more.

history: Lee-Weinberg bound (1977); 'WIMP miracle' nomenclature in 1990s. Dominant DM paradigm 1990s-2010s; LHC and direct-detection nulls have shifted attention to lighter (sub-GeV) and ultralight DM.

references.citation: Lee and Weinberg, 'Cosmological lower bound on heavy-neutrino masses', PRL 39, 165 (1977)
Bertone, Hooper, Silk, 'Particle dark matter: evidence, candidates and constraints', Phys. Rep. 405, 279 (2005)
